subject: Chemistry
title: The Reaction That Makes Steel
description: One-minute skill: The Reaction That Makes Steel.

hook.text: Iron is soft and rusts easily. Add a tiny amount of carbon and it becomes steel—stronger than almost anything.

buildup.text: For centuries, making steel was slow and expensive. Only swords and small tools justified the cost.

discovery.text: Henry Bessemer's 1856 process blew air through molten iron. Oxygen burned away excess carbon in minutes.

twist.text: Cheap steel transformed everything: railroads, skyscrapers, bridges, ships, cars, and canned food.

climax.text: The world produces 1.9 billion tons of steel per year. Civilization is literally built on this reaction.

punchline.text: Blow air through molten iron. Minutes later: steel. Cities followed.

quiz.question: What does the Bessemer process do to turn iron into steel?
options: Blows air through molten iron to burn away excess carbon
Adds more carbon to make iron harder
Cools iron rapidly in cold water
